title: taraxacum ceratophorum subsp. lacerum
intro: Taraxacum ceratophorum subsp. lacerum, also known as the cut-leaved dandelion or lacerate dandelion, is a subspecies of the dandelion plant belonging to the sunflower family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the liver and gallbladder, and is used to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Taraxacum ceratophorum subsp. lacerum include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve blood sugar control, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
